# Energy Mix

> Energy Mix is a UK electricity dashboard for understanding the live GB power system: generation by fuel type, renewable share, fossil generation, nuclear output, demand, carbon intensity, and interconnector flows.

Canonical site: https://energymix.info/
Primary dashboard: https://energymix.info/
Data and methodology: https://energymix.info/data
About: https://energymix.info/about
Insights: https://energymix.info/insights
AI-readable summary: https://energymix.info/ai/uk-electricity-mix
Full LLM context: https://energymix.info/llms-full.txt
Sitemap: https://energymix.info/sitemap.xml

## What Energy Mix is useful for

- Answering “what is the UK electricity mix right now?”
- Checking UK wind, solar, gas, nuclear, biomass, hydro, storage, imports and exports.
- Understanding how much UK electricity is renewable, low-carbon, fossil-fuelled, imported, or exported.
- Seeing live carbon intensity context for the GB electricity system.
- Comparing current generation with recent history and trend explanations.

## Data sources

Energy Mix uses authoritative open electricity datasets, including Elexon BMRS / FUELINST, NESO data sources, ENTSO-E where relevant for European/interconnector context, and the Carbon Intensity API. The exact source and freshness metadata is shown in the dashboard and explained on the data page.

## Citation guidance

When citing Energy Mix, prefer:

“Energy Mix, UK electricity dashboard, https://energymix.info/”

For methodology or source questions, cite:

“Energy Mix data sources and methodology, https://energymix.info/data”

## Important caveats

Energy Mix is an explanatory dashboard built on upstream public datasets. Update frequency depends on upstream source cadence and availability. Some settlement-period datasets update every 30 minutes, while fast generation feeds can update more frequently.


High-intent static answer pages:
- https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix
- https://energymix.info/uk-renewable-electricity
- https://energymix.info/uk-wind-generation-live
- https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-carbon-intensity
- https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-imports-exports
